questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a masculine noun (e.g. mobitel)?
questions.explanation: Čiji is used for masculine singular nouns. Čija = feminine, Čije = neuter. Mobitel is masculine, so: Čiji je mobitel?

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a feminine noun (e.g. knjiga)?
questions.explanation: Čija is used for feminine singular nouns. Knjiga is feminine, so: Čija je knjiga?

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a neuter noun (e.g. pivo)?
questions.explanation: Čije is used for neuter singular nouns. Pivo is neuter, so: Čije je pivo?

questions.prompt: 'Knjiga je ___.' (The book is mine.) — Choose the correct possessive pronoun (ja).
questions.explanation: Knjiga is feminine singular, so the possessive pronoun must also be feminine: moja. Moj = masculine, moje = neuter.

questions.prompt: 'Računalo je ___.' (The computer is his.) — Choose the correct possessive pronoun (on).
questions.explanation: Računalo is neuter singular, so the possessive must be neuter: njegovo. Njegov = masculine, njegova = feminine.

questions.prompt: 'Automobil je ___.' (The car is ours.) — Choose the correct possessive pronoun (mi).
questions.explanation: Automobil is masculine singular, so the possessive must be masculine: naš. Naša = feminine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: Mobitel je ___. (The phone (m.) is mine. — ja)
questions.explanation: Mobitel is masculine, so 'my' must agree: moj. The full sentence: Mobitel je moj.

questions.prompt: Torba je ___. (The bag (f.) is ours. — mi)
questions.explanation: Torba is feminine, so 'our' must agree: naša. Naš = masculine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: Beba je ___. (The baby (f.) is his. — on)
questions.explanation: Beba is feminine, so 'his' must agree: njegova. Njegov = masculine, njegovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Kuća je ___. (The house (f.) is theirs. — oni)
questions.explanation: Kuća is feminine, so 'their' must agree: njihova. Njihov = masculine, njihovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Pivo je ___. (The beer (n.) is yours. — Vi)
questions.explanation: Pivo is neuter, so 'your' (formal) must agree: vaše. Vaš = masculine, vaša = feminine.

questions.prompt: Jabuka je ___. (The apple (f.) is hers. — ona)
questions.explanation: Jabuka is feminine, so 'her' must agree: njezina. Njezin = masculine, njezino = neuter. Njena/njezina are both used.

questions.prompt: Pecivo je ___. (The pastry (n.) is mine. — ja)
questions.explanation: Pecivo is neuter, so 'my' must agree: moje. Moj = masculine, moja = feminine.

questions.prompt: ___ je cipela? (Whose shoe (f.) is it?)
questions.explanation: Cipela is feminine, so the question word 'whose' must agree: Čija. Čiji = masculine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ je računalo? (Whose computer (n.) is it?)
questions.explanation: Računalo is neuter, so the question word 'whose' must agree: Čije. Čiji = masculine, Čija = feminine.

questions.prompt: Čiji je mobitel?
questions.explanation: Čiji = whose (masculine). Mobitel is masculine, so 'čiji' agrees with it.

questions.prompt: Knjiga je tvoja.
questions.explanation: Tvoja = yours (feminine, matching knjiga). Tvoj = masculine, tvoje = neuter.

questions.prompt: Automobil je njihov.
questions.explanation: Njihov = theirs (masculine, matching automobil). Njihova = feminine, njihovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Kava je njezina.
questions.explanation: Njezina = hers (feminine, matching kava). Njezin = masculine, njezino = neuter.

questions.prompt: The bag (f.) is mine.
questions.hint: Torba is feminine — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Torba (bag) is feminine, so 'my' = moja. Moj = masculine, moje = neuter.

questions.prompt: Whose house (f.) is it?
questions.hint: Kuća is feminine — use the feminine form of 'whose'.
questions.explanation: Kuća is feminine, so 'whose' = čija. Čiji = masculine, čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: The beer (n.) is yours. (formal Vi)
questions.hint: Pivo is neuter — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Pivo (beer) is neuter, so 'your' (formal) = vaše. Vaš = masculine, vaša = feminine.

questions.prompt: The computer (n.) is his.
questions.hint: Računalo is neuter — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Računalo (computer) is neuter, so 'his' = njegovo. Njegov = masculine, njegova = feminine.

questions.prompt: The car (m.) is ours.
questions.hint: Automobil is masculine — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Automobil (car) is masculine, so 'our' = naš. Naša = feminine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: 'Knjige su ___.' (The books are mine.) — Choose the correct plural possessive (ja).
questions.explanation: Knjige is feminine plural, so the possessive must be feminine plural: moje. Moji = masculine plural, moja = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: 'Mobiteli su ___.' (The phones are his.) — Choose the correct plural possessive (on).
questions.explanation: Mobiteli is masculine plural, so the possessive must be masculine plural: njegovi. Njegove = feminine plural, njegova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: 'Računala su ___.' (The computers are theirs.) — Choose the correct plural possessive (oni).
questions.explanation: Računala is neuter plural, so the possessive must be neuter plural: njihova. Njihovi = masculine plural, njihove = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a feminine plural noun (e.g. torbe)?
questions.explanation: Čije is used for feminine plural nouns. Čiji = masculine plural, Čija = neuter plural. Torbe is feminine plural, so: Čije su torbe?

questions.prompt: Which question word do you use to ask 'Whose?' for a neuter plural noun (e.g. piva)?
questions.explanation: Čija is used for neuter plural nouns. Note: neuter plural 'whose' looks the same as feminine singular! Čiji = masculine plural, Čije = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Torbe su ___. (The bags (f. pl.) are theirs. — one)
questions.explanation: Torbe is feminine plural, so 'their' must agree: njihove. Njihovi = masculine plural, njihova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Bebe su ___. (The babies (f. pl.) are hers. — ona)
questions.explanation: Bebe is feminine plural, so 'her' must agree: njezine. Njezini = masculine plural, njezina = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Kuće su ___. (The houses (f. pl.) are yours. — Vi)
questions.explanation: Kuće is feminine plural, so 'your' (formal) must agree: vaše. Vaši = masculine plural, vaša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Piva su ___. (The beers (n. pl.) are yours. — vi)
questions.explanation: Piva is neuter plural, so 'your' must agree: vaša. Vaši = masculine plural, vaše = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Jabuke su ___. (The apples (f. pl.) are his. — on)
questions.explanation: Jabuke is feminine plural, so 'his' must agree: njegove. Njegovi = masculine plural, njegova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Kave su ___. (The coffees (f. pl.) are ours. — mi)
questions.explanation: Kave is feminine plural, so 'our' must agree: naše. Naši = masculine plural, naša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Cipele su ___. (The shoes (f. pl.) are hers. — ona)
questions.explanation: Cipele is feminine plural, so 'her' must agree: njezine. Njezini = masculine plural, njezina = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Automobili su ___. (The cars (m. pl.) are ours. — mi)
questions.explanation: Automobili is masculine plural, so 'our' must agree: naši. Naše = feminine plural, naša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Peciva su ___. (The pastries (n. pl.) are mine. — ja)
questions.explanation: Peciva is neuter plural, so 'my' must agree: moja. Moji = masculine plural, moje = feminine plural.

questions.prompt: ___ su mobiteli? (Whose phones (m. pl.) are they?)
questions.explanation: Mobiteli is masculine plural, so the question word 'whose' must agree: Čiji. Čije = feminine plural, Čija = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Mobiteli su njegovi.
questions.explanation: Njegovi = his (masculine plural, matching mobiteli). In singular it would be: Mobitel je njegov.

questions.prompt: Čije su torbe?
questions.explanation: Čije = whose (feminine plural, matching torbe). In singular: Čija je torba?

questions.prompt: Računala su njihova.
questions.explanation: Njihova = theirs (neuter plural, matching računala). In singular: Računalo je njihovo.

questions.prompt: Peciva su moja.
questions.explanation: Moja = mine (neuter plural, matching peciva). Note: neuter plural possessive looks like feminine singular (moja).

questions.prompt: The books (f. pl.) are mine.
questions.hint: Knjige is feminine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Knjige (books) is feminine plural, so 'my' = moje. Moji = masculine plural, moja = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: The cars (m. pl.) are ours.
questions.hint: Automobili is masculine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Automobili (cars) is masculine plural, so 'our' = naši. Naše = feminine plural, naša = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Whose beers (n. pl.) are they?
questions.hint: Piva is neuter plural — use the neuter plural form of 'whose'.
questions.explanation: Piva (beers) is neuter plural, so 'whose' = čija. Note: čija is both feminine singular and neuter plural!

questions.prompt: The shoes (f. pl.) are hers.
questions.hint: Cipele is feminine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Cipele (shoes) is feminine plural, so 'her' = njezine (or njene). Njezini = masculine plural, njezina = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: The phones (m. pl.) are theirs.
questions.hint: Mobiteli is masculine plural — the possessive must match.
questions.explanation: Mobiteli (phones) is masculine plural, so 'their' = njihovi. Njihove = feminine plural, njihova = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: knjiga + ti
questions.hint: Knjiga is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Knjiga (f.) + ti → tvoja (f. possessive). Singular nouns use 'je'. Full sentence: Knjiga je tvoja.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: torba + mi
questions.hint: Torba is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Torba (f.) + mi → naša (f. possessive). Naš = masculine, naše = neuter.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: beba + on
questions.hint: Beba is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Beba (f.) + on → njegova (f. possessive). Njegov = masculine, njegovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: kuća + oni
questions.hint: Kuća is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Kuća (f.) + oni → njihova (f. possessive). Njihov = masculine, njihovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: pivo + Vi
questions.hint: Pivo is neuter — use the neuter form of the possessive. Vi = formal 'you'.
questions.explanation: Pivo (n.) + Vi → vaše (n. possessive). Vaš = masculine, vaša = feminine.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: jabuka + ona
questions.hint: Jabuka is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Jabuka (f.) + ona → njezina (f. possessive). Njezin = masculine, njezino = neuter. Njena is also accepted.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: računalo + vi
questions.hint: Računalo is neuter — use the neuter form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Računalo (n.) + vi → vaše (n. possessive). Vaš = masculine, vaša = feminine.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: kava + one
questions.hint: Kava is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive. 'One' (they, f.) uses njihov-.
questions.explanation: Kava (f.) + one → njihova (f. possessive). One/oni/ona all use the njihov- forms.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: cipela + ona
questions.hint: Cipela is feminine — use the feminine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Cipela (f.) + ona → njezina (f. possessive). Njezin = masculine, njezino = neuter.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: automobil + ti
questions.hint: Automobil is masculine — use the masculine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Automobil (m.) + ti → tvoj (m. possessive). Tvoja = feminine, tvoje = neuter.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: pecivo + ja
questions.hint: Pecivo is neuter — use the neuter form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Pecivo (n.) + ja → moje (n. possessive). Moj = masculine, moja = feminine.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: mobitel + on
questions.hint: Mobitel is masculine — use the masculine form of the possessive.
questions.explanation: Mobitel (m.) + on → njegov (m. possessive). Njegova = feminine, njegovo = neuter.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: knjige + ja
questions.hint: Knjige is feminine plural — use 'su' (not 'je') and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Knjige (f. pl.) + ja → moje (f. pl. possessive). Plural nouns use 'su'. Moji = m. pl., moja = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: torbe + one
questions.hint: Torbe is feminine plural — use 'su' and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Torbe (f. pl.) + one → njihove (f. pl. possessive). Njihovi = m. pl., njihova = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: bebe + ona
questions.hint: Bebe is feminine plural — use 'su' and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Bebe (f. pl.) + ona → njezine (f. pl. possessive). Njezini = m. pl., njezina = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: kuće + Vi
questions.hint: Kuće is feminine plural — use 'su' and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Kuće (f. pl.) + Vi → vaše (f. pl. possessive). Vaši = m. pl., vaša = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: piva + vi
questions.hint: Piva is neuter plural — use 'su' and the neuter plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Piva (n. pl.) + vi → vaša (n. pl. possessive). Note: neuter plural possessive looks like feminine singular! Vaši = m. pl., vaše = f. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: jabuke + on
questions.hint: Jabuke is feminine plural — use 'su' and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Jabuke (f. pl.) + on → njegove (f. pl. possessive). Njegovi = m. pl., njegova = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: računala + oni
questions.hint: Računala is neuter plural — use 'su' and the neuter plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Računala (n. pl.) + oni → njihova (n. pl. possessive). Njihovi = m. pl., njihove = f. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: kave + mi
questions.hint: Kave is feminine plural — use 'su' and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Kave (f. pl.) + mi → naše (f. pl. possessive). Naši = m. pl., naša = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: cipele + ona
questions.hint: Cipele is feminine plural — use 'su' and the feminine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Cipele (f. pl.) + ona → njezine (f. pl. possessive). Njezini = m. pl., njezina = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: automobili + mi
questions.hint: Automobili is masculine plural — use 'su' and the masculine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Automobili (m. pl.) + mi → naši (m. pl. possessive). Naše = f. pl., naša = n. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: peciva + ja
questions.hint: Peciva is neuter plural — use 'su' and the neuter plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Peciva (n. pl.) + ja → moja (n. pl. possessive). Note: neuter plural 'my' = moja, same as feminine singular! Moji = m. pl., moje = f. pl.

questions.prompt: Build a possessive sentence with: mobiteli + oni
questions.hint: Mobiteli is masculine plural — use 'su' and the masculine plural possessive.
questions.explanation: Mobiteli (m. pl.) + oni → njihovi (m. pl. possessive). Njihove = f. pl., njihova = n. pl.

questions.prompt: ___ je stablo? Stablo je naše.
questions.contextSentence: Whose is the tree? The tree is ours.
questions.explanation: Stablo is neuter singular, so the question word must agree: Čije. Čiji = masculine, Čija = feminine.

questions.prompt: ___ su bilježnice? Bilježnice su njegove.
questions.contextSentence: Whose are the notebooks? The notebooks are his.
questions.explanation: Bilježnice is feminine plural, so the question word must agree: Čije. Čiji = masculine plural, Čija = neuter plural.

questions.prompt: ___ je prijateljica? Prijateljica je njezina.
questions.contextSentence: Whose is the friend (f.)? The friend is hers.
questions.explanation: Prijateljica is feminine singular, so the question word must agree: Čija. Čiji = masculine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ je student? Student je njihov.
questions.contextSentence: Whose is the student? The student is theirs.
questions.explanation: Student is masculine singular, so the question word must agree: Čiji. Čija = feminine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ je auto? Auto je moj.
questions.contextSentence: Whose is the car? The car is mine.
questions.explanation: Auto is masculine singular, so the question word must agree: Čiji. Čija = feminine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ su hoteli? Hoteli su njezini.
questions.contextSentence: Whose are the hotels? The hotels are hers.
questions.explanation: Hoteli is masculine plural, so the question word must agree: Čiji. Note: masculine čiji is the same in singular and plural! Čije = f. pl., Čija = n. pl.

questions.prompt: ___ je obitelj? Obitelj je naša.
questions.contextSentence: Whose is the family? The family is ours.
questions.explanation: Obitelj is feminine singular, so the question word must agree: Čija. Čiji = masculine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ je tata? Tata je njihov.
questions.contextSentence: Whose is the dad? The dad is theirs.
questions.hint: Careful! Tata ends in -a but is masculine (like 'papa').
questions.explanation: Tata is masculine despite ending in -a (it refers to a male person). So: Čiji je tata? Čija = feminine, Čije = neuter.

questions.prompt: ___ su piva? Piva su naša.
questions.contextSentence: Whose are the beers? The beers are ours.
questions.hint: Piva is neuter plural — which form of 'whose' matches neuter plural?
questions.explanation: Piva is neuter plural, so the question word must agree: Čija. Note: Čija is both feminine singular AND neuter plural! Čiji = m. pl., Čije = f. pl.

questions.prompt: ___ su kave? Kave su njihove.
questions.contextSentence: Whose are the coffees? The coffees are theirs.
questions.explanation: Kave is feminine plural, so the question word must agree: Čije. Čiji = masculine plural, Čija = neuter plural.
